response.justification: Bogdan is a Staff Software Engineer at Google with 15+ years of experience spanning embedded systems, high-throughput microservices, AI infrastructure, and full-stack development — making him severely overqualified for an entry-level junior Java role. While Java appears in his skills list and he used it briefly at Altom Consulting for robotics tooling, his primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang. More critically, the role explicitly requires strong German communication, and Bogdan self-reports only Basic German, which is a hard disqualifier for a client-facing consulting position in the German insurance sector.
strengths: Computer Science degree plus 15+ years of professional software development with demonstrated agile practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development)
Cloud experience via AWS CDK at Meta and Kubernetes platform work, satisfying the cloud technologies requirement
Proven ability to pick up new languages and paradigms quickly across web, mobile, embedded, and data-intensive systems
concerns: Grossly overqualified at Staff Engineer level — hiring him for a junior role creates near-certain attrition risk and likely salary misalignment
German is self-rated Basic, directly contradicting the mandatory strong verbal and written German requirement for client-facing insurance projects
Java is a footnote in a career dominated by TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang; no evidence of substantial Java project ownership or recent Java work
